opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was brushing my hair this evening and a huge clump just fell out and left a bald spot. I completely panicked — is something seriously wrong with me?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It happened so suddenly and I'm scared it's an infection or something spreading. I couldn't wait until morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: this afternoon, then the patch appeared this evening
symptom_narrative.duration: noticed over the last few hours
symptom_narrative.character: a smooth bald patch, no pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it doesn't hurt at all, but I'm really frightened
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on my scalp, towards the side
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: brushing my hair seemed to make more come out
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: maybe a bit of mild itch this afternoon before it happened, but no pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can do everything normally, I'm just very anxious about how it looks and what it means

family_history_as_reported.narrative: I think an aunt had a thyroid problem, not sure

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: been under a lot of stress at work recently

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told it is not cancer or a spreading infection

objective_data_offered.value: a round smooth bald patch about the size of a 50-cent coin, skin underneath looks normal, not red or flaky
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-shown on phone camera; lighting and angle limit assessment
